title: primula villosa subsp. cottia
intro: Primula villosa subsp. cottia, also known as the woolly primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the Primulaceae family. Traditionally, the woolly primrose has been used for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being. Woolly primrose is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be found in various herbal remedies and supplements.
